what was wrong with ham?
just really unclear wording as to what cards he can replay and how his rondino/crescendo works
his kit didn't translate well since they named his effects rondino and crescendo

because those terms in chinese are "repeating melody" and "rising melody" respectively, and then his stuff works based on whether or not the played cards have a "melody" effect (which covers both due to the naming)

but the english terms for them are rondino and crescendo, which are much better terms to use and fit well, but because of that change translating his skills doesn't make sense anymore so you end up being like "i don't get it, what's a melody"
I just removed that part and explicitly referenced rondino/crescendo so it makes more sense now

that and standardizing the terminology of non-derived cards since that's a standard phrase in the chinese that got translated really inconsistently across a bunch of kits which caused a fair amount of confusion regarding what cards thais/hameln effects can and can't work on
so hopefully those should be a lot clearer now in general

yeah, the chinese term is 非衍生 and is pretty commonly used throughout kits and cards

非 = non-
衍生 = derivative
okay finally

so non-derivative cards, i.e., base cards that are not derived cards, derived cards being cards that are created in some form by some mechanic or other skill that are not part of an awakener's base kit

For the problem for the English translation, if you want to arguably be more clear mechanically by saying *Repeat* instead of Rondino (because most players aren't music people and don't know what a Rondino is), you could literally have leave the crescendo (because laymen know what a crescendo is because of basic music classes)
Honestly, instead of Rondino, I personally would have use Da Capo, since this what personally most classical musicians use when repeating a piece.
